rag.type_desc.stats: Knowledge base record count and per-type breakdown
rag.type_desc.best_combination: Optimization runs ranked by gate reduction — filter by QPU or qubit count
rag.type_desc.suspicious_optimizations: Optimizations flagged for review: empty circuit, non-equivalence, or unverified 100% reduction
rag.type_desc.qec_results: Top QEC experiments by fidelity improvement — effective cases only
rag.type_desc.gpu_benchmark: GPU simulation speedup rankings — top CPU vs GPU acceleration results
rag.type_desc.pipeline_issues: Pipeline error log — failures and root causes per stage & SDK
rag.type_desc.noise_simulation: Calibration-based noise model results ranked by fidelity — best and worst circuit behavior on QPU noise
rag.type_desc.security_block: Static analysis blocks — dangerous imports or patterns detected in algorithm files
rag.meta.optimization.label: Optimizations
rag.meta.optimization.desc: Circuit optimization runs — gate/depth reduction results per combination & QPU
rag.meta.execution.label: Executions
rag.meta.execution.desc: QPU job execution records — shots, fidelity, job IDs, and result metadata
rag.meta.gpu_benchmark.label: GPU Benchmarks
rag.meta.gpu_benchmark.desc: CPU vs GPU simulation performance comparisons
rag.meta.pipeline_issue.label: Pipeline Issues
rag.meta.pipeline_issue.desc: Errors and failures encountered during pipeline stages, with root causes
rag.meta.transpile_pattern.label: Transpile Patterns
rag.meta.transpile_pattern.desc: Transpilation success/failure patterns and workarounds per SDK & QPU
rag.meta.qec_experiment.label: QEC Experiments
rag.meta.qec_experiment.desc: Quantum error correction experiment results — fidelity before/after encoding
rag.meta.qpu_availability.label: QPU Availability
rag.meta.qpu_availability.desc: QPU online/offline status snapshots over time
rag.meta.security_block.label: Security Blocks
rag.meta.security_block.desc: Requests blocked by static security policy — suspicious imports or patterns
rag.meta.conversion_pattern.label: Conversion Patterns
rag.meta.conversion_pattern.desc: Circuit conversion patterns between quantum frameworks (e.g. Qiskit ↔ TKET)
rag.meta.cache.label: Cache
rag.meta.cache.desc: Internally cached computation results for performance optimization
hint.opt.complexity: Gate count & depth reduction through transpilation to QPU native gate set and qubit topology
hint.opt.profile: T-ratio: T-gate fraction (costly on fault-tolerant hardware) · 2Q-ratio: two-qubit gate density (dominant error source on NISQ)
hint.opt.time: Optimize: unitary synthesis & gate cancellation · Mapping: SABRE/routing to QPU connectivity graph
hint.noise.fidelity: F = 1 − TVD · Fidelity near 1.0 means QPU noise barely perturbs the output distribution · TVD = max probability difference between ideal and noisy outcomes
hint.noise.dominant: Most frequently measured bitstring — if noise shifts this state, the circuit's computational result is corrupted
hint.gpu.speedup: Statevector simulation: GPU parallelizes 2ⁿ complex amplitudes simultaneously
hint.gpu.timing: Wall-clock time for full statevector evolution — measures actual hardware throughput difference
hint.qec.fidelity: F = 1 − TVD(ideal, noisy) — how well the QEC code restores the quantum state distribution corrupted by QPU noise
hint.qec.overhead: Physical resource cost of encoding — QEC requires redundant qubits & ancilla gates to detect/correct errors
hint.security.reason: Algorithm files run inside the QPU pipeline — unrestricted OS/network access could exfiltrate calibration data or manipulate results
hint.pipeline.analyze: Circuit extraction & gate-level analysis — failure here usually means syntax error or unsupported gate set
hint.pipeline.optimize: Transpilation to QPU native gate set + SABRE qubit routing — failure often means connectivity mismatch
hint.pipeline.noise: Calibration-based noise model simulation — requires successful transpilation to physical circuit first
hint.pipeline.qec_analyze: T2/fidelity threshold analysis to determine if QEC is needed for the target QPU
hint.pipeline.qec_apply: Logical-to-physical encoding with QEC code — increases qubit/gate count significantly
hint.pipeline.gpu: Statevector simulation on CPU vs CUDA GPU — GPU unavailability or OOM are common causes
hint.pipeline.qpu: Real QPU job submission — transpile errors, qubit count exceeded, or cloud API failure
